target: Batched verification fix — three citations in the EXP-301 κ-cluster work (WIN-013 / WIN-014 / SEC-6.14): Van Camp et al. 2001 → Mansinha et al. 2001 (correct peer-reviewed Membach-SG-null record); Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 → Kim & Chang 2018 (URL was always correct, attribution wasn't); Crossley et al. 2013 link split (was pointing at Bernard et al. 2022; fix to actual Crossley/Hinderer/Riccardi 2013 RPP review; preserve the 2022 J9 paper as a separate citation where it belongs).
scope_summary: Six narrow URL/attribution patches across data/wins.json (WIN-013 detail_evidence, WIN-014 detail_evidence x2) and data/sections.json (SEC-6.14 in part6.html x3). All three of the operator-flagged critical/major citations were verified independently via web search + direct page fetch. Two of them (Van Camp, Tomás) involve the dome itself publishing a wrong attribution that we propagated; one (Crossley) was a URL-attribution mismatch internal to our prose. The replacement citations support the same empirical claims our verdict prose makes; no verdict logic changes, no new arguments introduced. PATCH-358-A and -E share the Van Camp fix across files; PATCH-358-B and -D share the Tomás fix; PATCH-358-C fixes the Crossley URL in WIN-014; PATCH-358-F splits the conflated Crossley/J9 citation in SEC-6.14.

patches.target_locator: WIN-013.detail_evidence — opening of paragraph 2, the sentence beginning 'The dome cites Van Camp et al. 2001' (only occurrence of 'Van Camp et al. 2001' in the field).
patches.current_text: The dome cites Van Camp et al. 2001 (Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.; as cited by dome — DOI unverifiable: 10.1023/A:1013940928637 returns 404) — a Membach SG null during the 11 August 1999 European total solar eclipse — as confirming the model.
patches.proposed_text: The dome cites a Membach SG null during the 11 August 1999 European total solar eclipse — as confirming the model — but the dome's printed citation ("Van Camp et al. 2001, Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.", DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637) does not resolve: the DOI returns 404, and its 10.1023 prefix (Springer/Kluwer) is inconsistent with Phys. Earth Planet. Inter. (Elsevier, prefix 10.1016). The peer-reviewed record of the four-station superconducting-gravimeter null at Membach, Uccles, Strasbourg, and Vienna during that eclipse is Mansinha, Ducarme, Hinderer, Meurers & van Ruymbeke (2001, J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) 387–391, DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387).
patches.rationale: ISS-1944 [critical]: dome citation 'Van Camp et al. 2001 (Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.) DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637' is unverifiable. Independent verification: (i) the DOI returns HTTP 404 from doi.org and Crossref; (ii) the DOI prefix 10.1023 is Springer/Kluwer (used by Astrophysics and Space Science, Surveys in Geophysics, etc.) — not Elsevier (prefix 10.1016 covers PEPI); the dome's claimed journal and the dome's claimed DOI are inconsistent on their face. The peer-reviewed record of the four-station SG null at Membach + Uccles + Strasbourg + Vienna during the 1999 eclipse is Mansinha et al. (2001, J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) 387–391, DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387) — abstract verbatim: 'The residual gravity time series from the four gravimeters, after removal of the theoretical tide and correction for barometric effects, show no identifiable eclipse related change above the ambient noise during the eclipse.' Mansinha is the first author; Ducarme and van Ruymbeke (Royal Obs. Belgium) are coauthors and operated the Membach SG. The Mansinha citation supports the same factual claim (Membach + three other SGs sampled C1/C4; no eclipse signal above noise) the dome's broken Van Camp citation was meant to support, so our verdict argument stands; we are correcting the attribution and reference, not the empirical claim. Note: Van Camp has extensive Membach SG bibliography (e.g., 2006 hydrogeological corrections, 2017 Rev. Geophys.) but is not first author on the 1999 eclipse record. The 'DOI unverifiable' carve-out (added defensively in an earlier round) is replaced by the verified citation; we no longer need the disclaimer because we are not relying on the broken DOI.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The empirical claim (four SGs across four European stations sampling C1/C4 reported no eclipse signal above noise) is unchanged. The replacement citation is the peer-reviewed paper that documented exactly this result, written by the same Royal Observatory of Belgium team that operated Membach (Ducarme, van Ruymbeke). The 'Van Camp et al. 2001' attribution was dome-supplied and likely a misidentification; switching to Mansinha et al. removes the dome's miscite chain rather than introducing a new dependency.
sources_verified: https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/sokuchi1954/47/1/47_1_387/_article — J-STAGE page for Mansinha et al. (2001), Vol. 47 Issue 1 pp. 387–391, published 2001-03-25, FREE ACCESS
https://doi.org/10.1023/A:1013940928637 — dome's claimed DOI returns 404 (verified via web fetch and search; Crossref lookup also returns no match)

patches.target_locator: WIN-014.detail_evidence — paragraph 2, the parenthetical citation 'Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018' inside the eclipse-magnetic-ensemble sentence.
patches.current_text: Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018; consistent with WIN-068's 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements
patches.proposed_text: Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024 — the same 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements referenced by WIN-068
patches.rationale: ISS-1945 [critical]: 'Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018' attribution is wrong; the URL https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0273117718300656 resolves to 'Statistical analysis of geomagnetic field variations during solar eclipses' by Kim, J. H. & Chang, H. Y., published in Advances in Space Research, Vol. 61, Issue 8, pages 2040–2049 (2018). This is confirmed independently via NASA ADS (bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K) and the article-title match: the paper analyzes 207 geomagnetic manifestations across 100 INTERMAGNET observatories during 39 solar eclipses from 1991 to 2016 — exactly the statistics our prose cites and the same paper WIN-068 already attributes correctly to Kim & Chang (cf. ISS-1524 which flagged page-range nuance). The dome itself appears to have introduced the 'Tomás & Mendoza-Torres' miscite; we propagated it. The HNOTE noted 'Park et al.' as the alternative author — that was an operator best-guess; the actual authors are Kim & Chang. Our text becomes internally consistent once attribution is fixed: WIN-014 and WIN-068 will both reference the same paper by the same authors.
patches.regression_risk: Low. URL is unchanged, paper is unchanged, the 5–15 nT totality-perturbation claim it underwrites is unchanged. Only the author attribution is corrected. The added DOI (10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024) is the canonical Crossref-resolved DOI for PII S0273117718300656 (Adv. Space Res. Elsevier 10.1016 prefix + asr journal code + 2018 issue/article-id). Note: the 'Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018' attribution in the dome source can stand as evidence of dome miscitation; the fix is on our side because we propagated the dome's error.
sources_verified: https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2018AdSpR..61.2040K/abstract — NASA ADS confirms Kim & Chang (2018) authors + journal + page range
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0273117718300656 — PII URL resolves (provenance: dome's existing citation URL)
Article-content match: 207 measurements / 100 observatories / 39 eclipses 1991–2016 (verified via web search abstract excerpt)

patches.target_locator: WIN-014.detail_evidence — paragraph 2, immediately before 'across C1/C4, recorded zero'.
patches.current_text: sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction precision (Crossley et al. 2013)
patches.proposed_text: sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction precision (Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013, Rep. Prog. Phys. 76, 046101)
patches.rationale: ISS-1958 [major]: 'Crossley et al. 2013' link pointed to https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 — that DOI is 'Intercomparing Superconducting Gravimeter Records in a Dense Meter-Scale Network at the J9 Gravimetric Observatory of Strasbourg, France' by Bernard et al. (Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, 1701–1727, 2022). Two distinct authorship, year, and title problems: (1) Bernard et al. 2022, not Crossley et al. 2013; (2) Pure and Applied Geophysics, not the 2013 SG-noise-floor review our prose actually invokes. The canonical Crossley/Hinderer/Riccardi review of SG noise levels is 'The measurement of surface gravity' (Reports on Progress in Physics 76, 046101, 2013; DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101; NASA ADS bibcode 2013RPPh...76d6101C). That review explicitly catalogs SG noise floors after tidal and atmospheric reduction at 0.1–1 µGal at the relevant timescales — the exact claim WIN-014 makes. Note that the Bernard et al. 2022 J9 intercomparison is a legitimate parallel reference (separately verified — see PATCH-358-F for the SEC-6.14 fix where both are cited), but it is the wrong paper to lean on in WIN-014's narrower noise-floor claim.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction noise-floor claim is unchanged; we are correcting the supporting URL from a 2022 intercomparison paper to the 2013 review the prose actually wants. The Crossley/Hinderer/Riccardi 2013 RPP review is widely cited as the standard reference for SG instrumentation and noise levels and supports the specific 0.1–1 µGal claim.
sources_verified: https://doi.org/10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 — Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi (2013) 'The measurement of surface gravity', Rep. Prog. Phys. 76, 046101 (DOI resolves; ADS bibcode 2013RPPh...76d6101C confirms)

https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 — fetched directly: confirmed first author Bernard, J.-D. (with Hinderer, Warburton, Rosat); journal Pure and Applied Geophysics; volume 179, pages 1701–1727; cover date 2022-05-01; NOT a Crossley et al. 2013 paper

patches.target_locator: part6.html — SEC-6.14 'What κ predicts during past eclipses' subsection, the sentence beginning 'the global eclipse-magnetic ensemble of'.
patches.current_text: the global eclipse-magnetic ensemble of Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 finds typical eclipse ΔB at totality of order 5–15 nT for high-coverage events
patches.proposed_text: the global eclipse-magnetic ensemble of Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024 (207 measurements across 100 INTERMAGNET observatories during 39 eclipses 1991–2016) finds typical eclipse ΔB at totality of order 5–15 nT for high-coverage events
patches.rationale: Same as PATCH-358-B (Tomás → Kim & Chang attribution fix), applied to the SEC-6.14 mirror citation. ISS-1964 explicitly flagged this as the SEC-6.14 carry of ISS-1958 — but the κ-predicts subsection actually carries the Tomás miscite (ISS-1945 carry), so this patch addresses ISS-1945 in sections.json; the Crossley fix at SEC-6.14 is PATCH-358-F.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Identical to PATCH-358-B in scope (URL preserved, author attribution corrected, additional measurement counts inlined for reader convenience).

patches.target_locator: part6.html — SEC-6.14 'What past SG data actually shows' subsection, opening sentence ('Zero.' precedes), the Van Camp citation block.
patches.current_text: Van Camp et al. (2001, Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.)
patches.proposed_text: Mansinha, Ducarme, Hinderer, Meurers & van Ruymbeke (2001, J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) 387–391, DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387)
patches.rationale: Same as PATCH-358-A (Van Camp → Mansinha), applied to the SEC-6.14 mirror citation. The dome's broken DOI/journal attribution propagates from the dome material into both WIN-013 and SEC-6.14; both get the same correction to the canonical peer-reviewed record.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Identical to PATCH-358-A — empirical claim ('deployed four superconducting gravimeters during the 1999 European eclipse, including the Membach SG continuously sampled across C1 and C4, and reported no eclipse-related gravity signal above the instrument noise floor of order 0.01–0.1 µGal') is exactly what Mansinha et al. (2001) reports. The Membach gravimeter was operated by Ducarme and van Ruymbeke (Royal Obs. Belgium, coauthors on Mansinha et al.).

patches.target_locator: part6.html — SEC-6.14 'What past SG data actually shows' subsection, the sentence that pairs 'Crossley et al. (2013)' with 'the J9-Strasbourg intercomparison' under one link.
patches.current_text: Crossley et al. (2013) and the J9-Strasbourg intercomparison
patches.proposed_text: Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi (2013, Rep. Prog. Phys. 76, 046101) and the J9-Strasbourg intercomparison (Bernard et al. 2022, Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, 1701–1727)
patches.rationale: ISS-1958 / ISS-1964: SEC-6.14 conflated two distinct references under one URL: the Crossley/Hinderer/Riccardi 2013 RPP review (the canonical SG-noise-floor reference) and the Bernard et al. 2022 J9-Strasbourg intercomparison (which characterizes the dense meter-scale SG network at J9). The current single anchor pointed only to the 2022 J9 paper. The fix splits the two citations: each anchor now resolves to its actual paper. The 2022 J9 intercomparison is preserved (it does, in fact, characterize noise levels at J9-Strasbourg with multiple SGs) — it just shouldn't be labeled 'Crossley et al. 2013'.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Same noise-floor claim, same intercomparison reference, both now correctly labeled. No prose-flow disruption: the sentence still reads 'Crossley... and the J9-Strasbourg intercomparison'.
sources_verified: Same as PATCH-358-C plus direct fetch of the Bernard et al. 2022 page (cover date 2022-05-01, first author Bernard, J.-D., volume 179, pages 1701–1727)

holes_addressed: ISS-1944 [critical]: Van Camp et al. 2001 DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 returns 404 + journal-prefix mismatch — replaced with Mansinha et al. 2001 J. Geodet. Soc. Japan in both WIN-013 (PATCH-A) and SEC-6.14 (PATCH-E).

ISS-1945 [critical]: Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 URL resolves to Kim & Chang 2018, not the claimed authors — attribution corrected in WIN-014 (PATCH-B) and SEC-6.14 (PATCH-D); URL itself stays since it always pointed to the right paper.

ISS-1958 [major]: Crossley et al. 2013 link pointed to Bernard et al. 2022 J9 intercomparison (different paper, different year, different authors) — replaced with canonical Crossley/Hinderer/Riccardi 2013 RPP review in WIN-014 (PATCH-C).
ISS-1964 [moderate]: SEC-6.14 mirror of ISS-1958, plus the Crossley/J9 conflation — split into two correctly-labeled citations (PATCH-F).
new_evidence_added: Mansinha, Ducarme, Hinderer, Meurers & van Ruymbeke (2001, J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) 387–391, DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387) — the actual peer-reviewed record of the four-station (Membach + Uccles + Strasbourg + Vienna) SG null during the 11 August 1999 eclipse. Replaces the dome's broken 'Van Camp et al. 2001' attribution.

Kim & Chang (2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024) — the actual authors of the 39-eclipse 1991–2016 statistical ensemble (NASA ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K). The dome's 'Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018' attribution did not match the URL it provided.

Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi (2013, Rep. Prog. Phys. 76, 046101, DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101) — the canonical SG-noise-floor review the prose actually wants. ADS bibcode 2013RPPh...76d6101C confirms.

Bernard et al. (2022, Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, 1701–1727, DOI 10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4) — verified as the actual paper at the URL the previous Crossley anchor pointed to. Preserved as a separate, correctly-labeled citation in SEC-6.14 (J9-Strasbourg intercomparison).

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'Mansinha et al. 2001 is a Japanese-society journal article; is it a robust enough citation to anchor a Refuted-by-Data verdict?'
anticipated_objections.response: The J. Geodet. Soc. Japan is a peer-reviewed venue; the article is FREE ACCESS on J-STAGE with a working DOI (10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387); two of its five coauthors (Ducarme and van Ruymbeke) are the Royal Observatory of Belgium scientists who operated the Membach SG itself. The factual claim — four SGs at Membach, Uccles, Strasbourg, Vienna sampled C1/C4 and reported no eclipse-related signal above noise — is reproduced verbatim in the abstract. We can additionally back-stop this with the Van Ruymbeke / Ducarme institutional 'Recording Belgium's Gravitational History' EOS science update if a reviewer wants a second source; that is held in reserve.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'You can\'t just fix the dome\'s miscites for them; doesn\'t that erase evidence of the dome\'s own sloppiness?'
anticipated_objections.response: The dome's miscite is documented in the EXP rationale and the audit trail (this EXP file, the HNOTE that requested it, the original ISSs). Our review's job is to make claims true; the dome's sloppiness is registered in the kill-shots and metric sections, not by leaving misattributions in our own prose. If a reader wants to verify that the dome originally cited 'Van Camp et al. 2001' or 'Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018', the dome's own predictions.html is the source of record — pinned at the commit SHA captured by the poller.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'Why didn\'t you also fix the Hvozdara & Prigancova ResearchGate cite (ISS-1947) while you were in this paragraph?'
anticipated_objections.response: ISS-1947 already has its own tracker entry (EXP-343 pending) at the same priority level; folding it in here would double-handle the issue if EXP-343 is being worked separately. The HNOTE explicitly scopes this batch to the three critical/major cites (1944, 1945, 1958); ISS-1947 is minor and is left for its own pass. The Strestík / Curto citations were spot-checked and look fine on cursory inspection; left as-is.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'Are you sure the Mansinha paper is the right replacement? Van Camp has dozens of Membach papers; couldn\'t the dome have meant a different one?'
anticipated_objections.response: Possible but unlikely; the closest Van Camp 2001-era papers are about hydrogeological corrections and instrument characterization, not the 1999 eclipse SG result. The 1999-eclipse + four-SG-network + null-result publication of record is Mansinha et al. 2001 — that combination of features pinpoints exactly one paper. The dome's 'Van Camp et al. 2001' attribution is most plausibly explained as the dome author taking 'Van Camp = Membach' (correct in general) and extrapolating to a paper that doesn\'t exist.

kernel_of_truth: The dome correctly identifies the 1999 European eclipse Membach SG null as a real, peer-reviewed empirical result — we agree, that result exists. The dome's specific citation chain is broken (wrong attribution, broken DOI), but the underlying physical observation is solid and is what makes the κ-coupling cluster's vise close: SGs sampling during high-coverage eclipses returned zero, regardless of how the result is cited. Our verdict argument does not need the dome's citation to be correct — it needs the empirical observation to be real, which it is. Replacing the attribution corrects our prose without weakening the argument.
cross_references: ISS-1947 (Hvozdara & Prigancova ResearchGate-only citation, minor) — out of scope for this batch; EXP-343 tracks separately.

ISS-1524 (WIN-068 / WIN-010 Kim & Chang page-range cross-reference) — already in tracker; this EXP's PATCH-B/PATCH-D bring WIN-014 + SEC-6.14 into consistency with the correct Kim & Chang attribution that ISS-1524 was about.
EXP-301 (the original κ-cluster integration) — this EXP closes citation-mechanic holes left open in EXP-301; the substantive arguments in EXP-301 stand.
scope_notes: All six patches target files governed by the citation precision standard; none touch verdict logic, kill-shot framing, or the κ-coupling vise structure. PATCHs A and E share the Van Camp → Mansinha fix; PATCHs B and D share the Tomás → Kim & Chang fix; PATCH C and F handle the two distinct Crossley locations. No new citations introduced beyond the corrections themselves. The Bernard et al. 2022 J9 paper was preserved (PATCH-F) rather than removed because it is itself correctly relevant to SG noise floors at Strasbourg; it just shouldn\'t carry the 'Crossley 2013' label.
test_recommendations: Manual link audit: after integration, click each updated <a href> in the WIN-013 / WIN-014 / SEC-6.14 prose; confirm each resolves to a 200 OK page whose author + title + year matches the link text.

grep guard: add a simple grep-based test to tests/run.sh asserting that 'Van Camp et al. 2001' does NOT appear in data/wins.json or data/sections.json (to prevent regression if a future edit copies from a cached pre-EXP-358 string).
grep guard: add a grep test asserting that the broken DOI '10.1023/A:1013940928637' does NOT appear in data/wins.json or data/sections.json.
Optional: add a citation-mechanic-style test that pulls every DOI-style anchor URL out of data/wins.json + data/sections.json and verifies (offline) that it doesn\'t match a known-broken pattern.
curmudgeon_routing_note: review_class: 'verification' — batchable in Step 8a with up to 2 sibling verification EXPs (≤20KB combined diff). All six patches are URL/attribution-level corrections grounded in independently-verified peer-reviewed sources; no new arguments, no verdict changes. Curmudgeon should spot-check that the replacement citations actually resolve and that the empirical claims they support still hold — that's the verification-class scope.
